entry.title: Datasphere's task chain are not triggered after recent updates on HANA Cloud
entry.content:
Hello,

in the last week end an update of HANA Cloud under of Datasphere happens, according to this note https://me.sap.com/notes/3220622/E

We found that in many customers the scheduled task chains are not triggered.

The error seems random and not related to recent Datasphere upgrades about "scheduling with local timezone".

 

Did you experience this? 

are there any SAP notes?

thanks,

Alberto

entry.title: Re: Fiori Elements - Prerequisites SAPUI5 version
entry.content:
Try site 

https://sapui5.hana.ondemand.com/

this has option to change the version as per the attachment, you can look into by changing version .other wise you can look into what each version has 

https://sapui5.hana.ondemand.com/sdk/#/topic/6660a59a8a22460a91fe65a0d96a567e

entry.title: SAP Service and Asset Manager 2305+ : send push notification when Work Order is unassigned from user
entry.content:
Hi SAP Community

For SAP Service and Asset Manager 2305+, there is the requirement to send push notification to iOS device when a Work Order is unassigned from end user.

My questions are:

- Can this functionality be achieved with out of the box SSAM 2305 version and higher?

- If customizations are needed, are the steps below making sense?

1. define custom ABAP class handler for PUSH oMDO, with custom assignment logic (to query the subject user)

2. under configpanel, specify the above Z class to the PUSH oMDO and PUSH scenarios

3. under configpanel, specify desired fields and filters for the PUSH exchange object

Any inputs and comments are appreciated.

Thanks, Jinlin

entry.title: Cannot log into AI Launchpad
entry.content:
Hi 

I am having trouble logging into AI Launchpad. I am following this: Use Boosters for Free Tier Use of SAP AI Core and SAP AI Launchpad | SAP Tutorials because I want to attempt this: Chatbot Development Made Easy with SAP AI Core and Build Apps 

I've done everything exactly the same using the free tier of BTP (not trial) and even tried other regions for the sub account. When trying to access the launchpad, I get to the login screen which says I cannot be authenticated. When I click forget password it doesn't send me a reset link leading to me thinking it can't find an account with my details.

entry.title: Re: Allowing SAP Support to logon to BTP services when Co...
entry.content:
Hi Andrew

For some services the parameter "?sap_idp=sap.default" added at the end of the URL works. This works for Workzone  (see note https://me.sap.com/notes/2823487/E) and HANA Cloud for example but does not work for Integration Suite or Business App Studio.

Another important point is that depending on how you have configured your principal propagation the default IDP might not work. This specific IDP uses mail as mandatory attribute for login name so if you're using a different attribute in your scenario it seems not possible to configure.

Hope to hear other thoughts from the community on this.

Diego

entry.title: Re: Generate CAP Project in BAS with Error @sap/hdi-deploy@5.4.2 Not Match
entry.content: If we execute npm install -g hana-cli it still gives error

entry.title: Re: Saving / opening a model (PDM) takes far too long
entry.content: Thanks for the hint. Maybe this a part of the problem. Unfortunately I am not able to test on dedicated hardware.

entry.title: Re: What configuration need to CHECK PRINT in SAP S4HANA...
entry.content:
Hello dear user,

And thank you for trusting the SAP Community to ask your question here.

An answer has already been brought to your issue in the link below :

Check Printing in SAP S4 Hana Cloud 

Could you please check it and see if it solves your issue ?

Best regards,

Jeremy

entry.title: Re: There is an API for calling GETPDF for a billing Docu...
entry.content:
Hello,

In the API documentation, you can find the information that this is expected behavior:

"When a billing document has multiple output items containing a PDF, the service always fetches the newest PDF across all output items in output channels PRINT and EMAIL."

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/03c04db2a7434731b7fe21dca77440da/990f1ce2078f42e4b593d6d060966bd8.html?locale=en-US

I do not think that there is a simple way to change this functionality, especially in the public cloud. 

Best regards

entry.title: Re: EDI PO to SO questions - How to remove segment E1EDKA...
entry.content: Sorry I thought by only entering the material number it should be enough but indeed it needs to be entered in the PO in field IDNLF or in the Info Records. Now I am getting the qualifier correctly determined 🙂

entry.title: Re: SAP for Me Portal Latency Issues Again
entry.content: Hi, was there any solution to this? It took me 15 mins and still the page is stuck on 'loading'... other websites open in a split second..

entry.title: Re: Planned order is not getting created for Assemble to...
entry.content:
Hello

Can you please check if SAP Note 3124967 is implemented in your system? If the note is already implemented, then you should open an incident to SAP, as there is a known side-effect of this note that might trigger this error message incorrectly.

Best regards,
Caetano

entry.title: Inside SAP's Own Procurement Transformation with SAP Ariba Category Management
entry.content:
By Lisa Heinrich, Senior Solution Value Advisor, Finance and Spend Management, SAP

I recently tuned in to the Procurement Leaders’ session, “Leveraging AI for Category Management,” featuring Nouh Abed Assad, COO of SAP’s Global Procurement Organization, hosted by Ian Lawless from Procurement Leaders and joined by Barri Horn, SAP’s Director of Product Marketing. I have to say—it was one of the most energizing and eye-opening sessions I’ve attended in a while.

What made it so compelling? It wasn’t a sales pitch. It was SAP pulling back the curtain on how we’re using our own SAP Ariba Category Management tools to transform procurement from the inside out. The honesty, the detail, and the practical insights captured and held my interest from beginning to end.

Nouh shared SAP’s journey methodically and with clarity, showing how a category-driven model supported by AI is helping SAP’s business shift from tactical execution to strategic impact. What resonated most with me was his emphasis on category management as a “living, breathing process”—not just a one-time exercise. It’s about aligning with business goals, understanding market shifts, and tracking outcomes that matter.

Any strong sourcing and procurement organization should have a category plan. But when Nouh described how AI-powered tools like generative segmentation and market dynamics analysis are now part of SAP’s daily procurement playbook, I got excited. These tools aren’t just flashy extras. They help category managers challenge assumptions, spot opportunities, and build smarter, faster strategies that they can share with their stakeholders. As Nouh put it perfectly: “Savings come from doing the right work; AI just helps us do it smarter.”

He also underscored a critical point relevant to adopting new tools: planning, change management, and procurement best practices are non-negotiables. Without structure and team buy-in, even the best tools often fail to gain traction with business users. His advice? “You should have implemented it yesterday.” That one stuck with me—I’ve wanted to say that to so many customers I’ve worked with over the years. Honestly, I’ve even thought about putting it on a bumper sticker!

As someone in SAP’s Solution Value Advisory team, I work closely with customers every day help them get the most out of SAP Ariba solutions to drive adoption. Seeing the very strategies and tools I champion brought to life within SAP itself was incredibly validating. Whether I’m leading workshops or helping businesses define their solution value road map, I often point to SAP Ariba Category Management as a critical addition for long-term procurement success.

Why This Matters

Category management—especially when powered by AI—isn’t just about cost savings. It’s about unlocking long-term value, supporting sustainability goals, and strengthening collaboration across the business. This session reinforced how real that value is and how accessible it can be when strategy, technology, and people align.

If you’re pondering where to begin, these tips may help:

  • Standardize your approach.
  • Invest in change management.
  • Start small—but start NOW.

SAP’s story isn’t just a vision—it’s already a rapidly evolving reality. And after watching this session, I’m more energized than ever to help others get there, too. 

Now it’s your turn! Register here today to watch this informative session on demand.

entry.title: Re: Time Series (Trend) chart
entry.content: You could clean past data values which do not seem to be relevant and add additional inputs (note that you need to have values input for the future periods). The model is just sometimes bad because there is no pattern in data. Here in your example, I guess it just proposed a lag1 model.

entry.title: Re: How to activate CDS views that depend on each other?
entry.content:
I have solved the issue by creating a program that forces the CDSs to activate.
You are welcome to read the blog I wrote.

Force mass activation of CDS with dependencies 
https://community.sap.com/t5/application-development-and-automation-blog-posts/force-mass-activation-of-cds-with-dependencies/ba-p/14061133

entry.title: Report for Transfer Stock - In-Plant
entry.content:
Hi Team, 

Considering the fiori app Transfer Stock - In-Plant, we have a report for we can check the information of the moviments?

MB52 is just for movement 313, when the material appears in transit.

entry.title: Re: Read archived records from DFKKZP table
entry.content:
This table should be archived with object FI_MKKPAYL (AOBJ, SARA)

  • There is a reload program RFKKZA02
  • Look for documentation at the (ADK) Archive Development Kit - Archive Development Kit BC-CCM-ADK 
  • Look at sample program SBOOKR (read archived data)

entry.title: Re: Remote Code Analysis in ATC – Technical Setup step by step
entry.content:
Hi @KumarAakash ,

why not to use remote ATC?

Kind Regards,
Olga.

entry.title: Re: Inside SAP's Own Procurement Transformation with SAP Ariba Category Management
entry.content: I'm looking into the issue with the URL and apologize for the inconvenience.

entry.title: Re: Not able to get the workflow instance tasks in my BPA...
entry.content:
There could be several reasons why you are not able to get the workflow instance tasks in your SAP Build Process Automation (BPA) inbox. Here are some steps to help you troubleshoot the issue:

1. Verify Access to My Inbox
Ensure that you are accessing My Inbox correctly based on your role:(https://help.sap.com/docs/PROCESS_AUTOMATION/a331c4ef0a9d48a89c779fd449c022e7/d5ecf81f85874a0d83294bc94d8a69b7.html)

Citizen Developers: Access My Inbox from the My Inbox icon in the Lobby. For more information, see
My Inbox for Citizen Developers (https://help.sap.com/docs/PROCESS_AUTOMATION/a331c4ef0a9d48a89c779fd449c022e7/d5ecf81f85874a0d83294bc94d8a69b7.html)

Process Participants: Access My Inbox from the My Inbox tile in your SAP Build Work Zone home page. For more information, see 
My Inbox for Process Participants

2. Check Task Assignment
Ensure that the tasks are assigned to you. You can view all tasks assigned to you, claim tasks, and process them in My Inbox. For more information, see (https://help.sap.com/docs/PROCESS_AUTOMATION/a331c4ef0a9d48a89c779fd449c022e7/d5ecf81f85874a0d83294bc94d8a69b7.html)

3. Monitor Workflow Instances
Use the Process and Workflow Instances view to see a list of all workflow instances and act on those instances. For more information, see (https://help.sap.com/docs/PROCESS_AUTOMATION/a331c4ef0a9d48a89c779fd449c022e7/de40f56efdff44a09724480367803b8b.html)

4. View Tasks of Workflow Instance
To view the tasks of a workflow instance, choose Show Tasks in the instance details. For more information, see
More Options (https://help.sap.com/docs/PROCESS_AUTOMATION/a331c4ef0a9d48a89c779fd449c022e7/85d7fc5a28584a7d86cc90a86a8a610c.html)

5. Ensure Proper Configuration
Ensure that the workflow is properly configured and deployed. Follow the steps for configuring and deploying the project in a productive environment. For more information, see (https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_INTEGRATED_BUSINESS_PLANNING/feae3cea3cc549aaa9d9de7d363a83e6/74a34f8c5bf64c81a5ed13e7f0909be1.html)
Configuring SAP Build Process Automation

entry.title: Re: SAP Advantage ODBC drivers
entry.content: I have all drivers for all version. What do you need ?

entry.title: Re: error message: enter a valid currency when running Re...
entry.content:
Finally, the problem was that last time the user run the report he chose a currency that after another user deleted from the "Currencies" table in SAP. So, when the user tried to run the report he could not because the currency of the report was deleted.
The solution was to create the currency again in the "Currencies" table and after that he was able to run the report again.

entry.title: Re: Time Series (Trend) chart
entry.content: @SophieElgharib"The model is just sometimes bad because there is no pattern in data" by this you mean to say forecast works well only when there is a definite pattern in the data? I am just trying to understand. And what is lag1 model? I dont get it.

entry.title: Re: SAP Build Process Automation: Error importing project...
entry.content:
To resolve errors encountered when importing a project from another BTP sub-account in SAP Build Process Automation, follow these steps:

  1. Check Prerequisites:

    • Ensure that the project has been exported correctly from the SAP Build Process Automation lobby or SAP Intelligent RPA Studio. The project should be saved as a ZIP file on your local system 
      (https://help.sap.com/docs/PROCESS_AUTOMATION/a331c4ef0a9d48a89c779fd449c022e7/ca98da78eb1f4d329d82b66ef8baac98.html?locale=en-US&state=PRODUCTION&version=Cloud)

  2. Import the Project:

    • You can import a project either from your local system or choose from the list of available projects (https://help.sap.com/docs/PROCESS_AUTOMATION/a331c4ef0a9d48a89c779fd449c022e7/ca98da78eb1f4d329d82b66ef8baac98.html?locale=en-US&state=PRODUCTION&version=Cloud)

  3. Troubleshoot Automations:

    • If you experience issues related to automations, thoroughly analyze the traces and check your project. Refer to the troubleshooting guide for automation (https://help.sap.com/docs/PROCESS_AUTOMATION/a331c4ef0a9d48a89c779fd449c022e7/6626409193b84c0cbf8dc0ce996da593.html)

  4. Export Health Metrics:

    • Export health metrics for your agent to identify any underlying issues 
      (https://help.sap.com/docs/PROCESS_AUTOMATION/a331c4ef0a9d48a89c779fd449c022e7/36593c5253764792b998d6b5e9f60551.html)

  5. Update Destinations for Live Content Packages:

    • If you already use SAP Build Process Automation and SAP Workflow Management and have configured SAP Build Work Zone, update the destination to use Live Content Packages 
      (https://help.sap.com/docs/PROCESS_AUTOMATION/a331c4ef0a9d48a89c779fd449c022e7/26dbc5ab6b6041f0a02c1c1211611c97.html)

entry.title: Re: Determine Partner from one Bill To party to Multiple...
entry.content:
Hi @kirankumardara, 

It seems there may be a bit of context missing from your description—are you using a multiple customer scenario (e.g. 0S02)? And are your condition records maintained at the Sold-To level with different rebate rates?

If your pricing needs to reflect different rates per Sold-To, but the contract is created at the Bill-To level, then you’ll need to ensure the Sold-To Party is passed to the Settlement Document line items (WBRP).

How to achieve this: 

  • You can do this by using Split Criteria to transfer the Sold-To from the business volume to the line item level of the settlement document.
  • Then, adjust your Access Sequence to include the relevant field where the Sold-To is stored (e.g., custom field or standard if available).
  • Alternatively, there are other ways to influence the pricing determination, such as Transfer Manager logic or custom enhancements, depending on your system setup and flexibility needs.

Regards, 
Gauthier VICTOR

entry.title: Cannot delete custom Business Function Set - SFW3 (switch framework)
entry.content:
I have created a Business Function Set ZZZ via SFW3, which I used to activate a custom switch (via a business function). Now I want to delete it, the business function is deactivated in SFW5:

But I obtain this error message when deleting:

Deletion is not possible
Business Function Set ZZZ is currently activated system-wide

How can I deactivate it system-wide?

entry.title: Re: How can we get a TADIR entry for any given object in...
entry.content:
Hello @100361466722 ,

Did you try to check from Eclipse-> Open ABAP Development Object-> TADIR? if  you are unable to open and check from TADIR, you must be able to directly check/display the object from Open ABAP Development object. Hope this helps.

Best Regards,

Maciej

entry.title: Re: Build Process Automation API Trigger, Can't Get Defin...
entry.content:
Ensure you have a service key from the SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP). For more information, see
Create a Service Key for the SAP Build Process Automation Instance


Procedure ( https://help.sap.com/docs/PROCESS_AUTOMATION/a331c4ef0a9d48a89c779fd449c022e7/5bdc85433a944b0db47bd3907812c0d8.html?locale=en-US&state=PRODUCTION&version=Cloud )
Access Control Tower: In SAP Build, choose Control Tower, then select the Environments tile

Select Environment: Select your environment and go to the Triggers view

Add Trigger: Choose Add Trigger, select a project, and choose Next

Select API: Choose API and Next

Configure Trigger Settings: Configure the trigger settings and optionally, the agent attributes. Note that the maximum expiration time is limited to 30 days or 4 weeks. Once the expiration time is passed, the trigger appears in Expired status

Create Trigger: Choose Create. You can now copy the API trigger information and close

Testing the API Trigger
Once the API call is set as your process trigger, you can test the call using Postman. For detailed instructions, ( https://help.sap.com/docs/PROCESS_AUTOMATION/a331c4ef0a9d48a89c779fd449c022e7/7ba3a9894ec140368d7c6e944c9ddcbc.html?locale=en-US&state=PRODUCTION&version=Cloud)

Additional Information
ISO 8601 Format: Ensure date and time are in the ISO 8601 format: YYYY-MM-DD (e.g., 2023-03-16) and hhss (e.g., 1516)

entry.title: Gmail Integration with SAP Build Process Automation (RPA)
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I'm working on a custom automation in SAP Build Process Automation (BPA) using the Gmail features from the Google Workspace SDK. My goal is to automate data extraction from Gsheet file attached in Gmail. However, I ran into an issue right at the beginning, when trying to search for the email, the automation fails with the following error:

Error: Please specify user email Id
Exception: irpa_core.error.SequenceError

#Configuration

  • I'm using a Google service account key for authentication.

  • I call the "Google Authorization" activity at the start of the automation. I provided the userEmail generated in the Service Account key, and the authorization is successfully executed without any issue.

  • Search Email query parameter : "subject:" + Step0.searchSubject + " AND from:" + Step0.searchFrom.

  • Domain-wide delegation is configured in Google Admin Console :

   - https://mail.google.com
   - https://www.googleapis.com/auth/drive
   - https://www.googleapis.com/auth/gmail.modify
   - https://www.googleapis.com/auth/cloud-platform

Has anyone experienced this? Could there be an internal requirement to pass the userEmail again within the Gmail task itself?

Any help, guidance, or shared experience would be greatly appreciated 🙏
Thanks in advance!
Kais

entry.title: Re: Fiori elements: returning values when doing intent ba...
entry.content:
Hi Robert,

Since Fiori Elements, let us use onAfterSave for smooth back navigation. (link)

this.editFlow.onAfterSave(function() {
    sap.ushell.Container.getService("CrossApplicationNavigation").toExternal({
        target: { semanticObject: "AppA", action: "display" }
    });
});

to be redirected to App A after saving in App B.

Thanks,

Jakes

entry.title: Re: Cannot delete custom Business Function Set - SFW3 (sw...
entry.content:
Go to SFW5, unselect "Business Function Set" and click the button "Activate Changes" as shown here:

Now you can delete the custom business function set.

NB: for information, the current business function set is stored in the table SFW_ACTIVE_B1, with version = "A". If the concerned set is still in this table, the error message is sent.

entry.title: Re: How to enable texts to be displayed on the purchase order outputs?
entry.content:
@SivaKumarSeelam Thanks for that explanation.

I have a question regarding the deletion of settings: Is it possible to the delete entries from SSCUI 104689 (Define Texts for Contract)?

We are currently printing some notes fields that should only be internal, however i see no possibility to revert that entry.

Best regards,
Kerisha

entry.title: Re: Fiori elements: returning values when doing intent ba...
entry.content:
and with parameters:-

var oCrossAppNav = sap.ushell.Container.getService("CrossApplicationNavigation");
oCrossAppNav.toExternal({
    target: { semanticObject: "AppA", action: "display" },
    params: {
        objectId: "12345",
        draftId: "67890",
        mode: "edit"
    }
});

entry.title: Re: How to create an event in SAP Build Automation to cal...
entry.content:
To create an event in SAP Build Process Automation that calls a REST iFlow in the cloud, follow these steps:

Prerequisites
Ensure the following prerequisites are met: (https://help.sap.com/docs/PROCESS_AUTOMATION/a331c4ef0a9d48a89c779fd449c022e7/9e171a2f1af943438167fb1ba194c862.html?locale=en-US&state=PRODUCTION&version=Cloud)

Your SAP S/4HANA Cloud system is available and connected with SAP Build Process Automation, and is able to receive all required notification events. For more information, see
Enable the Consumption of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Events

You have set up an SAP Event Mesh service instance in your SAP BTP cockpit subaccount. For more information, see
Setting Up SAP Event Mesh in BTP Cockpit

You have created your queue and your queue subscriptions. For more information, see
Manage Queues

You have created a webhook subscription to subscribe to an event queue. For more information, see
Manage Queue Subscriptions

You have created a service key. For more information, see
Create a Service Key for the SAP Build Process Automation Instance

Steps to Create an Event Project (https://help.sap.com/docs/PROCESS_AUTOMATION/a331c4ef0a9d48a89c779fd449c022e7/cb133f94c32d44a690c1718a73008d06.html?locale=en-US&state=PRODUCTION&version=Cloud)
Create an Event Project:

Use a unified customer landscape to consume APIs from SAP applications, such as SAP SuccessFactors, SAP Ariba, and SAP S/4HANA Cloud, that are registered with a global account in SAP BTP

Configure the Webhook URL:

Fulfill the Webhook URL by using the URL from the instance of the SAP Build Process Automation plan standard Service Key api endpoints and adding internal/be/v1/events. The webhook URL should be as follows: plaintext https://spa-api-gateway-bpi-eu-prod.cfapps.sap.hana.ondemand.com/internal/be/v1/events
Insert the Client ID, Client Secret, and Token URL by adding oauth/token to the latter. Copy and paste these credentials from the service key

Create a Consumer Queue:

Create a consumer queue with appropriate topic subscriptions to capture which events from SAP Integration Suite, Advanced Event Mesh reach SAP Build Process Automation. For more information, see
Configuring Queues (https://help.sap.com/docs/PROCESS_AUTOMATION/a331c4ef0a9d48a89c779fd449c022e7/623365f5e0b24e8c9d500ffe37f36a83.html?locale=en-US&state=PRODUCTION&version=Cloud)

Create a Custom REST Delivery Point:

Use the information in the event to call the API and start the business process

Create a REST Consumer:

Inside the new REST connector, which requires the credentials from an SAP Build Process Automation service instance. For more information, see
Creating a REST Connector

Create a Queue Binding:

Enter the POST Request Target to create a queue binding.
Enter the Request headers if created, so that the queue binding won't fail.
Enter the Content-Type, for example, application/cloudevents+json.
Enter the string that identifies the SAP Integration Suite, Advanced Event Mesh instance in WebHook-Request-Origin, for example, "aem, prod-aem"

Next Steps
You can consume the published events in your process. For more information, see
Create an Event to Start a Process (https://help.sap.com/docs/PROCESS_AUTOMATION/a331c4ef0a9d48a89c779fd449c022e7/11f18c7c07c14cde953af17f8521ff6f.html?locale=en-US&state=PRODUCTION&version=Cloud)


By following these steps, you can successfully create an event in SAP Build Process Automation to call a REST iFlow in the cloud.

entry.title: Re: How to activate CDS views that depend on each other?
entry.content:
Hi Hauke,

Marking a view as "experimental" sometimes bypasses dependency issues(link)

@ObjectModel.usageType.serviceQuality: #X
define view Z_CompositionGrandchildView as select from Z_CompositionChildView { ... };

Thanks,

Jakes

entry.title: Re: Instance Feature Control of a field in Action button...
entry.content:
Hello @ankahit ,

Could you clarify are you trying use Action: Features - Instance ? or you can directly write your logic in custom save functionality.

Best Regards,

Maciej

entry.title: Where can I learn processes and tools for effective sourcing within the SAP S/4HANA environment?
entry.content:
I recommend to access our Exploring Sourcing in S/4HANA learning journey.

Overview
This course provides participants with an overview of the sources of supply available in SAP S/HANA, and presents the processes and tools necessary for effective sourcing within the SAP S/4HANA environment.

Learning objectives
​After completing this learning journey, Business Users will be able to use special purchasing information records, the source list, and quota arrangements, and describe the different options for determining the source of supply.
This course equips users with the skills needed to perform sourcing processes and drive organizational efficiency.

Prerequisites 

  • Exploring Contract Management in SAP S/4HANA Sourcing and Procurement

Please ask a question related to the digital learning Journey in the Q&A area. 
Our SAP Learning Experts will get back to you as soon as possible! We are here to support you. 

I appreciate your feedback and we will make sure to continue sharing interesting topics.

Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: Re: Best Approach for Scheduled Trigger with User Credent...
entry.content: This solution is actually very viable, since the user can change the credential as he wishes without interrupting the flow. Thank you Manish !

entry.title: Re: Joule Setup / Error during Booster Execution
entry.content:
Hi Lukas,

When you setup Joule for the Public Cloud, you work everything within one BTP sub-account. You will create one Joule and one Work Zone instance under the sub-account. When you have entitlements in two separate accounts, how can you put instances under one sub-account?

I haven't seen this error message before, seems quite complicated. Can you try to work everything under one sub-account first?

Regards,

George

entry.title: Re: ATP & MRP Areas
entry.content: Hello @michael_thinschmidt , Thanks for the feedback , the flag is deactivated and the system anyway didn't allow both flags to be activated together

entry.title: Re: SAC - currency conversion across three currencies
entry.content:
@eric2357 

Currently FX Rate triangulation is not supported as pointed by you. There is a influence request for this feature but its under long term consideration so no set date for availability. You can upvote it so that it gets enough votes and traction to be picked up by Product development team.

Derive exchange rate for missing currency combination from a different combination in currency table

Improvement Request Details - Customer Influence

Hope this helps !!

Nikhil

entry.title: Re: Can we Provide an Object For Attribute as Associated...
entry.content:
Hello @Anoop_L ,

If I understand the question right, you want to store the object reference of a class in an attribute? If this is the case then yes you can. Instantiate the object within the class and pass the reference to the attribute defined.

Best Regards,

Maciej

entry.title: ‌🇯🇵‌ Workshop: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Go-Live Experience (Partners Only)
entry.content:
Are you ready to elevate your SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition skills? Join us for the Go-Live Experience Workshop, designed exclusively for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition partners. This hands-on workshop offers a unique opportunity to engage in an implementation project within a safe and supportive environment.

Workshop Highlights:
- Hands-On Implementation: Gain practical experience with key activities, tools, and resources essential for successful customer implementation projects.
- Target Audience: Ideal for partners in delivery or consulting roles, this workshop is tailored to enhance your expertise and readiness for real-world projects.
- Participation Details: We invite three participants from each partner to join us. The fee is €200 per participant.
- Language: Japanese. 

What to Expect for Localization:
- Introduction to SAP S/4HANA Cloud Localization
- Accessing Information on the Japan Version
- Self-Service Localization Tools

More information about the workshop you can find here.

How to Register:
To secure your spot, please send your nominations to satoshi.watai@sap.com. If you are registering on behalf of a colleague, ensure to include their details in your nomination.

Don't miss this chance to deepen your understanding and prepare for future customer engagements!

If you want to collaborate with SAP experts on localization topic in Japan, join:
- Japan - Enterprise Resource Planning Localization Workzone Group
- Japan Payroll - Human Capital Management Localization Workzone Group
- Japan — SAP Document and Reporting Compliance Workzone Group

entry.title: Re: Report for Transfer Stock - In-Plant
entry.content: You have a standard report mb5t

entry.title: Re: Empty profit center from billing documents gets overw...
entry.content:
Hi @IlseVercruysse, 

Great to see you continuing your journey with SAP CCM—always good to have familiar names contributing in this space!

Regarding your issue: yes, I believe this behavior is consistent with standard SAP logic. 

Please refer to SAP Note 2074289, which explains how and when default values are pulled in during document creation, including from the material master or plant settings. This behavior can impact consistency between delta accruals and final settlement, especially when the billing data lacks a profit center but the system fills one in during settlement processing.

Recommendations:

  1. Check Why the Profit Center is Missing in Billing Documents:
    It’s likely a gap on the OTC (Order-to-Cash) side, where profit center determination during billing is incomplete. Addressing this upstream will help avoid inconsistencies later in Settlement Management.

  2. About Transfer Event 65:
    If you're trying to "force" a blank profit center using Transfer Manager, note that inserting ' ' (a space) will not have the desired effect. In the Transfer Manager, a truly blank value must be passed—not a string with a space character, as ' ' is interpreted as a literal value, not as empty.


Regards, 
Gauthier VICTOR

entry.title: Operazioni del ciclo di produzione phantom
entry.content:
è possibile inserire, all'interno del ciclo di produzione, delle operazioni phantom? Se si, in che modo?
Ho questa necessità perchè il cliente, nel ciclo di produzione inserisce delle operazioni "spente" che devono essere baypassate. 
Il problema si pone in quanto SAP deve essere integrato con un MES il quale però non fa differenze fra le operazioni e quindi, una volta importato l'ordine, andrebbe a lavorare anche l'operazione spenta.

entry.title: Re: How to create an IM PI document for a SLOC which is t...
entry.content:
To create a physical inventory count for an empty SLOC, use MI09 (Create Inventory Count without Reference). Ensure the new material and batch exist in the SLOC. Enter the plant, SLOC, material, batch, count date, and count class. Perform the count and post differences using MI10.
This method avoids dummy materials and simplifies the process.

Create a Physical Inventory Count:
Go to MI09 (Create Inventory Count without Reference).
Enter the Plant and Storage Location (SLOC).
Add the material number and batch (if applicable).
Enter the count date and count class as needed.
Perform the Inventory Count:
Record the actual stock count in the system.
Post the Inventory Difference:
Use MI10 to post the inventory differences. This will update the stock levels accordingly.

entry.title: Re: UI for SAP Build Apps / Apps Editor looks different
entry.content: Hi, thanks for the feedback. Would you be able to share what tutorial you're referring to? We will check if there are updates scheduled. Thanks.

entry.title: Re: Log changes in Qunats
entry.content: Execute transaction /SCWM/MON from where you can navigate to Product Master Data > Display Change Documents. Alternatitively, you can also explore /SCWM/PROD_CD_READ which is material specific

entry.title: Permission "Manage Compensation Plan" missing & No chance to create new Comp Plan
entry.content:
Dear all,

I activated Comp & VarPay in provisioning and selected (as far as I am aware) all permissions for Comp & VarPay. I still cannot find the above mentioned permission "Manage Compensation Plan" in Permissions.

When go for "Compensation Home" only "add new Variable Pay Plan" is available (see screenshot below), no Comp or Total Compensation Plan available.

Do you maybe have a fix for my problem?

BR

Michael

entry.title: Re: While creating PR using tcode ME51N an error showing...
entry.content:
Hi @SouravChowdhury 
Seems users are doing copy-paste the details in ME51N, It is accepted. but sometimes 'Item text' field or any other free text field will not accept - If the characters starting with special symbols and other reasons..
If the above situation is not valid, Please verify the material master text (MAKTX - if any special symbols exists) also check 'Basic data text' if it exists.
Please update us with your observation.

entry.title: Re: Empty profit center from billing documents gets overw...
entry.content:
Hello Gauthier,

many thanks for your answer. The requirement of the customer is to have an empty profit center on these specific billing documents.  I will implement the transfer event as you suggested. So i understand that instead of putting ' ' , nothing should be put in the Fixed value field. 

Thanks again for your valuable input!  

Best regards!

entry.title: Where can i learn how to implement SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Global Benefits?
entry.content:
I recommend to access our Configuring SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Global Benefits learning journey.

Overview
This learning journey will guide you through the configurations necessary to implement SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Global Benefits.

Learning objectives
After completing this learning journey, you will be able to perform and configure the core functionalities within SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Global Benefits and access the resources and support available to configure additional features.

Prerequisites 

  • Exploring SAP SuccessFactors Platform

Pass all the quizzes and receive a digital badge

Please ask a question related to the digital learning Journey in the Q&A area. 
Our SAP Learning Experts will get back to you as soon as possible! We are here to support you. 

I appreciate your feedback and we will make sure to continue sharing interesting topics.

Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: How to backup transactional data of SAP BPC 10.1 on SAP BW/4HANA Starter Add-On
entry.content:
Hello Community,

I'm looking for advice on how to back up SAP BPC 10.1 transactional data on the SAP BW/4HANA Starter Add-On. I've read blogs written on SAP and tried implementing the suggested methods, but unfortunately, they didn't work for me.

One approach I'm considering is creating a field-based ADSO and moving data from the BPC cube to the ADSO. I would appreciate any other suggestions or approaches you might have!

Hi All,

I'm getting this error while doing PGI process.

Kindly suggest how to resolve it

Regards

entry.content: will not work because /SCWM is not available in our system. But thank you anyway

entry.content: adding tag SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Sales as CDS views belong to this LOB Area

entry.title: Re: Log changes in Qunats
entry.content: Thank you. But I searched for a user exist in case it exists. Do not want to change the standard function here

entry.title: Re: Log changes in Qunats
entry.content: In case there is no user exit I will reject this business requirement

entry.title: Re: Personal information change in RCM
entry.content: It depends. If the data the candidate is updating is part of the candidate application template, then yes, it will update the active application. However, if it is in the candidate profile, it will not have any impact on existing applications. Ideally, the candidate will have edit permissions for the candidate application fields only at the initial or default stage. Once they are moved past that stage, edit permissions will not be granted, and the candidate will not be able to modify what they have already updated. When using a multistage application, you can decide until what stage the candidate should have edit access to the application fields.

entry.title: Re: IC Charging between Cost Centers with different currencies using SAP Analytics Cloud
entry.content:
@Mathias_Venker 

Thanks for the write up. Just a small clarification, the Advanced Formula to read the respective Converted measure to LC based on the partner CC, why cant be the IF condition simply be on partner cc currency check. Any CC charging to partner CC with GBP should be charged in GBP LC irrespective of the charging CC. Am I missing anything ?

 

IF [d/P_CostCenter].[p/Curr] = "GBP" THEN
DATA([d/Account] = "IC_CHARGES_IN", [d/CostCenter] = [d/P_CostCenter].[p/ID], [d/P_CostCenter] = [d/CostCenter].[p/ID], [d/Measures] = "VALUE_LC") = RESULTLOOKUP([d/Measures] = "VALUE_GBP")

ENDIF

Nikhil

entry.title: Re: Interview Scheduling with Calendar Integration in SAP Fieldglass
entry.content:
Hi Keith, 

In a scenario where the calendar integration is not enabled, it is still possible to add the interview event to the interviewer's calendar.

When messaging for the Interview / Meeting Schedule module is enabled, it allows the message recipient to generate an ics file which can then be used to add the event to their calendar.

Hope this answers your question?

entry.title: Re: SAP Convergent Charging - Setting Up Core Tool User interface
entry.content:
Hi Francois,
thanks for the message. I checked the version and it looks as below. After I moved the sapmachine-jre-17.0.14 to the path C:\Program Files\Java\, and editing it on the Core tool script, I was able to login to CC. But this on the windows server where SAP CC 2023 is installed.



Now I am having an issue on my laptop 🙂 Same way sapmachine-jre-17.0.14 was moved to C:\Program Files\Java path and I get this: 

The Core Tool script looks as below: 


Here are also the system variables: 


But, strangely, I get the below error and normally cannot access the core server: 

Any hint what to check?
Thanks a lot and have a great day!
Doris

entry.title: Re: BP Configuration – Same BP Number for Customer and Vendor.
entry.content: Thanks for sharing ! Nicely explained.

entry.title: Re: Not getting all fields displayed in the fiori output...
entry.content:
Hello @Ramana23 ,

I guess you have only used UI.lineItem annotations to display the table but you have to explicitly add annotations for object page. Creation of header, Facets would help load all the fields in object page.

also you can give importance to the fields which can help you display the only important columns in main page and rest will be available for addon selection/expansion. Here is an example that can help.

 

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-q-a/object-page-in-fiori-list-report-is-blank-column-header-and-data-from-cds/qaq-p/13734640

Best Regards,

Maciej

entry.title: Re: Can we set mandatory for attachments
entry.content: AnjaniAL, The entity typcically can only be edited from the Detail Form for the Entity. Is there another form that you are looking to prevent executing the entity edit from?

entry.title: F5104, Intercompany billing EDI
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

I need help in configuration of the automatic posting for intercompany invoices via EDI.

Idoc failed with error message F5104 "vendor XXX ist not maintained in company zzz".

customizing in OBCA, OBCB, OBCC, OBCD, OBCE ist done.

Vendor ID in error message is the same as G/L-account in OBCB. 

Can anyone help to fix this?

Thanks in advance

Cornelia

entry.title: Re: Generate CAP Project in BAS with Error @sap/hdi-deplo...
entry.content: It was because @SAP/hdi-deploy didn't have version 5.4.2 published and cds-dk was trying to install that particular version. I see now that the version is published by @SAP/hdi-deploy in npm registry everything seems to be working.

entry.title: Re: IC Charging between Cost Centers with different currencies using SAP Analytics Cloud

you are probably right, will test it. I just started with the combination where sender and receiver currency is the same, so Value in Local Currency could be simply used. For that I needed to check both sides and I stayed with the "logic". 😁

Thanks, Mathias

entry.title: Re: Error no accounts maintained for company code clearin...
entry.content: Customizing transaction is OBYA

entry.title: Re: SAP Integration Suite - How to execute POST using OData Adapter?
entry.content: Hi Pavan,

entry.title: how to make change request as rejected as there is no action
entry.content:
We have a typical situation.  We have a change request that has incident and normal change.  Incident is set to cancelled and Normal change is withdrawn. 

Now the status of Change request is in Release for approval.  But the approval status is in rejected status. At this point we don't have any action to perform.  How to set this change request as rejected. 

 

Thanks,

Raghu

entry.title: Re: SAP Integration Suite - How to execute POST using OData Adapter?
entry.content: As suggested by Pavan, " As an alternative you can try creating a simple mapping using generated edmx file in the target structure to post data. " Perform this. and the issue gets resolved. Since the payload we are sending the the payload IBP is expecting is different. But EDMX file will help us to create the target structure through mapping. And I am able to send the payload to IBP.

entry.title: Re: F5104, Intercompany billing EDI
entry.content: For which version of SAP : ECC, EHP, S/4HANA, cloud - public - private ?

entry.title: DVM Cloud App values vs DBACOCKPIT (HANA DB Memory Usage)
entry.content:
Hello SAP community,

We are trying to figure out how does DVM Cloud Application on launchpad (me.sap.com) calculates HANA Memory Usage (GB).

In DBACOCKPIT on the system, we can see:

Memory used/Allocation Limit (MDC), which should be the whole memory used for the HANA system - 357,22GB.

DVM Cloud Application shows Hana Memory Usage (GB):

Used - 583GB. How is this number calculated? 

Free - 369GB. How is this number calculated? 

We can see that Fast Rest Memory + Memory Used (tenant) = 349GB + some memory for overhead processes = Total Memory used in DBACOCKPIT. 

If the DVM Cloud App shows 583GB MEMORY USED, my guess is, it is showing also part of the persistance layer (data on disk). Basically it is Memory Used by HANA system - 357.22GB + (disk) ? If so, why is it called "HANA Memory Usage (GB)" ? .. 

If we take a look at the disk usage in dbacockpit:

Data Volume Size - 536,56GB

and compare it with DVM Cloud App:

Used 431 GB .. Fragmented 106 GB .. = 431 + 106 = 537 which is roughly the same as values from DBACOCKPIT.

If we check disk usage by executing SQL command SELECT * FROM M_DISK_USAGE;

USED_SIZE DATA is roughly the same as HANA Memory Usage in Cloud APP (the execution of the command was few days later).

 

USAGE_TYPEUSED_SIZEFILE_SIZE
CATALOG_BACKUP20,48020,480
DATA568,485,437,440568,479,186,944
DATA_BACKUP-1-1
LOG61,204,643,84061,204,643,840
LOG_BACKUP20,48020,480
ROOTKEY_BACKUP-1-1
TRACE498,139,136461,623,547

Best Regards

entry.title: Re: F5104, Intercompany billing EDI
entry.content: sorry, S/4HANA on premise

entry.title: Re: F5104, Intercompany billing EDI
entry.content:
Hello,

The message seems to be quite straightforward - check if the supplier mentioned in the error is created in the company code in which you post the invoice. 

Best regards

entry.title: Re: IC Charging between Cost Centers with different currencies using SAP Analytics Cloud
entry.content:
@Mathias_Venker 

Got it, Also for the first check may be its more dynamic to just test if charging cc and charged cc have common LC

IF [d/CostCenter].[p/currency] =  [d/P_CostCenter].[p/Curr]  THEN

DATA([d/Account] = "IC_CHARGES_IN", [d/CostCenter] = [d/P_CostCenter].[p/ID], [d/P_CostCenter] = [d/CostCenter].[p/ID]) = RESULTLOOKUP()

ENDIF

Nikhil

entry.title: Identity Authentication Activation in Build Work Zone for SAP Joule (SUFA)
entry.content:
Hi together,
to activate SAP Joule in SuccessFactors (SUFA) as described in the Discovery Center mission, I need to enable Identity Authentication in SAP Build Work Zone. Unfortunately, I'm currently blocked due to access and configuration issues.

Here’s what has happened so far:

  • The SuccessFactors instance is successfully connected in a separate BTP Global Account.

  • I’ve subscribed to SAP Build Work Zone in my BTP Subaccount.

  • The trust to the IAS tenant is set up and active.

  • I manually created my user in IAS (with the same email address), but in the BTP cockpit, a second user appears – still without access to the “Identity Authentication” tab in Work Zone.

  • An admin account with access to the tenant also doesn’t see the necessary tab.

  • “Create Shadow Users During Logon” is enabled in the trust configuration.

I suspect that either the original service subscriber is not correctly linked in IAS, or there’s a mapping issue between users and the IAS setup.

Here’s the mission I’m working on:
https://discovery-center.cloud.sap/protected/index.html#/mymissiondetail/152454/card/11573701/?tab=projectboard

I’d appreciate support or a recommended workaround to proceed with the activation.

Thanks and best regards,
Lucas Maier

entry.title: Re: CMS components extending SimpleCMSComponent are showi...
entry.content:
Hi @Harish_Vatsa 

It would be good to see the behaviour of the system in action (as always), but I have the feeling that it might be a sync issue. SmartEdit relies on the staged and online catalog versions. Therefore, ensure that the components are properly synchronised. Did you try to manually synchronizing the affected components from Backoffice (Catalog > Synchronization)?

If that is not the root cause for your issue, it can be also related to authorisations. SmartEdit has specific visibility rules. Even if the component is active in Backoffice, it may not appear in SmartEdit if it lacks certain permissions. Check the user roles and permissions for the SmartEdit user (especially for CMS components).

Finally, consider checking the console in your browser for any Javascript related errors. With that regard also consider to clear the browser cache and refresh the page.

 

I hope one of my thoughts points you in the right direction.

entry.title: Re: Log changes in Qunats
entry.content: The question is about LE-WM, not EWM and about quant changes, not product changes.

entry.title: Re: Log changes in Qunats
entry.content: There is no user exit for that. You would need to enhance the standard function.

I appreciate any reply.

Thank you

 

Kind regards

Jhon Sebastián.

entry.title: Re: Can sqlViewName of CDS view be renamed?

entry.title: Re: Permission "Manage Compensation Plan" missing & No ch...
entry.content:
I found the solution myself. I needed to add the permissions to create form templates from "Manage Form Templates". After getting the permission it worked for me.

 

I am still a little bit confused why VarPay worked out but Comp did not

entry.title: Announcement: Removal of Belize Theme of SAP Fiori
entry.content:
Executive summary

  • Automatic move to Horizon theme in SAP Fiori
  • Action is needed for custom themes based on Belize.

Themes in SAP Fiori

SAP Fiori is a design system that provides a consistent user experience for SAP software across platforms and devices. It is at the core of SAP’s product experience and design strategy. An essential element defining many aspects of the visual design is the theme. The latest Horizon theme is an evolution of the SAP Fiori design system and offers a modern, fresh look, and an improved user experience helping users to focus on their daily work. 

Belize theme removal

A previous version of Horizon is the Belize theme, introduced with SAP Fiori 2.0. Belize is deprecated as of SAPUI5 1.120 (see Deprecated Themes and Libraries) and SAP recommends to move to the current Horizon theme.

 

Existing usages of Belize will be automatically redirected to the Horizon theme.
With SAPUI5/OpenUI5 1.136 the UI5 controls will not work any longer with the Belize theme.

For more details on Belize in SAP S/4HANA and other products, please see Upcoming Removal of SAP Fiori themes Belize and Blue Crystal from @ThomasReiss. Other UI technologies are affected in the same way:

  • Web Dynpro ABAP: SAP_UI 8.16 & SAP_BASIS 9.16
  • SAP GUI for HTML: SAP Kernel 9.16 used in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition 2025 and SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2508
  • WebClient UI: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition 2025 and SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2508

With this focus on the Horizon theme SAP can deliver innovations faster. Also, runtime performance is expected to gain from a smaller CSS payload.

Custom themes based on Belize

Using the UI theme designer customers can apply their own branding by adapting a standard SAP Fiori theme: Customers may replace the logo or change the main branding colors of the UI by replacing the default brand and base colors of the theme. When creating a custom theme, an SAP standard theme (such as Morning Horizon) is selected as base theme.

Existing custom themes based on Belize need to be migrated before a mentioned UI technology version is used. Details on this migration are given in Migrate Your Themes with Ease and SAP Help: Migrating Themes on SAP BTP, Cloud Foundry environment.

entry.title: Upcoming Removal of SAP Fiori Themes Belize and Blue Crystal
entry.content:
The SAP Fiori visual themes Belize and Blue Crystal have been deprecated for many years. Now the time has come to optimize our UI technology by removing these themes. We plan to remove them in the next releases of products which allow users to choose multiple themes, such as SAP S/4HANA Cloud and SAP S/4HANA (on-premise) and SAP Integrated Business Planning.

Over the years SAP Fiori has evolved, introducing new visual themes along the way – most recently the Horizon theme. The Horizon theme not only looks modern and appealing, it also provides the highest level of support for accessibility, as well as the most flexibility for customers to create their own custom themes.

Horizon is the visual theme used by all our products in the SAP Business Suite. Figures 1 – 3 below show some examples of Horizon in our products.

We strongly recommend that all users use the Horizon theme to ensure an optimal and consistent user experience.

 

Removal of Belize and Blue Crystal

The visual themes Belize and Blue Crystal will be removed from SAP products with their next releases, starting in the second half of 2025. This means that the upcoming releases of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition and SAP Integrated Business Planning, as well as further products which currently include one or both of these themes, will no longer provide them as options.

After upgrading, existing usages of Belize or Blue Crystal will be automatically redirected to the Horizon theme.

If you have created your own custom theme based on Belize or Blue Crystal using the UI theme designer, you will need to migrate this to an available theme. Our strong recommendation is to base the new version on the Horizon theme.

For more information about the UI technologies affected, and how to migrate custom themes, have a look at this blog post by Oliver Graeff:

  • Announcement: Removal of Belize theme of SAP Fiori.

Additional Information for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

Important: If you use a custom theme and have set up the UI Theme Designer connection to your SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition with a communication arrangement on SAP Business Technology Platform Neo, make sure to move it to Cloud Foundry before you upgrade. Otherwise the automatic redirect to Horizon will not work. Also, future innovations will require the communication arrangement to use Cloud Foundry, so doing this move makes sense anyway. Here is a link to the documentation for this:

  • Integrating UI Theme Designer – SAP BTP Cloud Foundry Environment.

 

Examples of SAP Fiori visual theme Horizon

You can see some examples for Horizon in figures 1 – 3: for SAP Start and SAP Mobile Start, for SAP S/4HANA Cloud (the images show SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502) and SAP SuccessFactors. For further examples, have a look at these blog posts:

  • SAP Fiori Update: More Products with Horizon, Entry Point strategy, UX news for SAP S/4HANA and More.
  • SAP User Experience Update: Yet More Products with the New Look driven by SAP Fiori.
  • Improvements Coming to the Horizon Visual Theme for SAP Fiori.
  • SAP UX Innovations in Public Cloud Products, Joule, AI, Entry Points, Apple Vision Pro and more.
  • SAP UX Latest 2024 Innovations in Public Cloud: Joule, GenAI and More.
  • SAP User Experience Q1/2025 Update – Part 1: Many New Innovations Available (AI, Joule and More) – along with parts 2 – 7 which are linked therein.

Figure 1: The SAP Fiori visual theme Horizon for SAP Start and SAP Mobile Start. ALT Text: the image shows a screenshot of SAP Start with the Horizon theme, three iPhones with Horizon (one dark and one light showing SAP Mobile start, one showing an iPhone start screen with a notification from SAP Mobile STart ), and an Apple Watch with a notification from SAP Mobile Start and Horizon.

Figure 2: The SAP Fiori visual theme Horizon for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. ALT Text: an image showing two sceens with Morning Horizon (the My Home page and Monitor Purchase Order Items analytical list page) and two screens with the same apps with Evening Horizon, plus the text “Also available for accessibility: high contrast light and dark themes”.

Figure 3: Examples of SAP SuccessFactors applications with the SAP Fiori visual theme Horizon. ALT Text: Three examples showing both web and mobile: on the left the Home Page, in the middle Team Goals, on the right Opportunity Marketplace showing many cards, each displaying an opportunity.

To find out more about the Horizon theme, have a look at the SAP Fiori for web design guidelines:

  • Theming.

entry.title: Re: F5104, Intercompany billing EDI
entry.content:
Have you checked the vendor number in the IDOC? Most probably, some steps in customizing are incorrect. 

What number do you have in KNVV-EIKTO field in the customer master data?

entry.title: How can we easily calculate how much the parameter value for abap/heap_area_dia can be set to?
entry.content:
Dear SAP Community members,

How can we easily calculate how much the parameter value for abap/heap_area_dia can be set to? Also does this have impact on how much the "dia", "non-dia" and total values need to be set?

Regards

entry.title: Related Request for Change (ChaRm UI field) not appearing in search criteria but available in View
entry.content:
Hi All,

As we checked, Related Request for Change (ChaRm UI field) not appearing in search criteria but available in View section. Please find screenshot below.

Related request for change field is not available under search criteria's all available fields. Please find screenshot below.

We also checked in ChaRM CRM UI settings. Field ( Related Request for Change) itself is not available under field configuration. Thus, Search-relevance can not be made as search & result list type field.

Regards, 
Parag 

#SOLMAN Change Control Management   @Richard_Büffing

entry.title: Re: Step-by-Step Guide to SOAP Integration in SAP CPI
entry.content: nice job Abhishek, well explained and detailed 🙌

entry.title: Encapsulation of Crystal Reports 8.5 library in Windows service
entry.content: Good morning, we are currently still looking for a reporter to migrate to Crystal 8.5, we are waiting for authorization to migrate to a more current version of Crystal, but at the moment we are having a problem and that is because we have an application that uses this version of the reporter that runs at x64 bits, we had to encapsulate the Crystal Reports 8.5 library in a Windows service, without the viewer, that is, we only generate the pdf through the service and the Crystal library and then with a pdf viewer we show it, to avoid using the viewer, now the doubt that arises due to an inconvenience that we are presenting is that sometimes when doing the . cr.OpenReport(strRpt, CRAXDRT.CROpenReportMethod.crOpenReportByTempCopy) the service falls, I think but I'm not sure and I would like to ask you if this encapsulation needs to take something into account since I suspect that when making that .openReport it seems that the dll tries to use the gui

entry.title: Re: F5104, Intercompany billing EDI
entry.content: Hello Stanislaw, thanks for your quick answer. The Idoc mentioned the correct supplier number in E1EDKA1 RS. Any other ideas? Thanks in advance.

entry.title: Couldn"t find transport requests in BW bridge (Eclipse)
entry.content:
When connecting via Eclipse to ECC and BW (through the BW Bridge), I am unable to locate any Transport Requests specifically for the BW Bridge. However, transport requests for ECC are visible under the Transport Organizer within BW Bridge. This issue is critical, as I need visibility into BW Bridge transport requests for effective development and management.

Required Assistance:
Please help clarify or modify the Eclipse configuration to ensure BW Bridge transport requests are visible within the Transport Organizer.
